summary: Gruber shares his first impressions of AirPods Pro after a hands-on briefing in New York, testing them across subways, trains, and city streets. He finds the noise cancellation remarkably effective, comparing it favorably to his Bose over-ear headphones and calling the difference from regular AirPods 'night and day.' He highlights the clever force sensor that mimics a physical button click, the useful Transparency mode, and thoughtful software touches in iOS Control Center. He confirms battery life matches Apple's 4.5-hour claim and finds them comfortable and secure after three hours of continuous use.
key_points: Noise cancellation is so effective it blocks out subway noise, train rumble, and household appliances, making AirPods Pro useful even when playing nothing
The force sensor creates an uncanny clicking sensation through audio feedback alone, exemplifying Apple's attention to detail in mimicking physical interactions
Transparency mode solves the sealed-ear problem by letting ambient sound through selectively, useful for conversations and street awareness
The Ear Tip Fit Test intelligently determines whether your current tip size provides a good seal, and can recommend different sizes per ear
Battery life precisely matches Apple's 4.5-hour claim based on real-world testing across three hours of continuous use
money_quotes: The difference is like night and day. Amtrak trains are pretty noisy — especially at what we in the U.S. so adorably consider 'high speeds' — but with AirPods Pro the clackety-clack rumble was effectively blocked out.
It's so good you really do lose sense of your surrounding aural environment.
As someone who doesn't generally write while listening to music, I'm likely to use AirPods Pro, playing nothing, just to tune out the world around me in a noisy space.
They actually have more of a premium clicky feel than the truly clicking buttons on Apple's wired EarPods, even though they don't actually click. It's uncanny, and Apple at its best.
It's a simple little interaction done exquisitely well.

The AirPods Pro case is about 15% larger by volume than the regular AirPods case. That's unfortunate, but it's not noticeable in a regular pants pocket, and it still fits in the fifth pocket of a pair of Levi's 501 jeans.
I've never had a problem with regular AirPods falling out of my ears, but AirPods Pro feel way more secure.
key_insight: AirPods Pro deliver noise cancellation good enough to rival over-ear headphones while showcasing Apple's signature attention to interaction design in details like the force sensor's phantom click and the iOS control interface.
